anchor_list.excerpt: This is a classifier for evaluating mathematical reasoning and deduction in web pages, fine-tuned from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small.

description_variants: The FineMath classifier is a multilingual-e5-small-backed classifier; intfloat/multilingual-e5-small is the base encoder finetuned for the math-quality regression head.
FineMath classifier is fine-tuned from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small as a regression scorer for mathematical reasoning content.

The FineMath classifier is fine-tuned from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small with a single-output regression head; both Phase 1 (3-point scale) and the released Phase 3 (5-point scale, score 0-5) versions used multilingual-e5-small as the initialization.
FineMath classifier is a multilingual-e5-small-based classifier finetuned for math-quality regression.

FineMath classifier is initialized from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small with a regression classification head added on top; embedding and encoder layers were frozen during fine-tuning, so only the classification head's parameters were updated.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/finemath-classifier
edges.description: The FineMath classifier's training labels are LLM-judged quality scores produced by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (3-point scale in Phase 1, refined 5-point scale in Phase 3).

anchor_list.excerpt: we used [Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct](https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct) to generate annotations on a 3-point scale... Once again, we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale
anchor_list.explanation: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct generates the educational-quality annotations the FineMath classifier is trained on.

anchor_list.excerpt: we used Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct to generate annotations on a 3-point scale ... we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale ... We finetuned a new classifier on these annotations
anchor_list.explanation: Annotations used to train the classifier are produced by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct.

anchor_list.excerpt: we trained a mathematical content [classifier] using annotations generated by LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct ... we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale ... We finetuned a new classifier on these annotations.
anchor_list.explanation: Source states that Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct produced the per-page math-quality annotations that became the classifier's training labels.
anchor_list.position: ## What is it? (line 154); ## Dataset curation, Phase 3 (line 213)

anchor_list.explanation: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct produced the annotations on which the FineMath classifier was finetuned.

description_variants: The FineMath classifier's training labels are LLM-judged quality scores produced by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (3-point scale in Phase 1, refined 5-point scale in Phase 3).
Training labels for the FineMath classifier (math-quality scores on 3- then 5-point scales) were generated by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct annotating CommonCrawl/OpenWebMath-extracted pages.

Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct generated the math-educational-value annotations (3-point scale in Phase 1, 5-point scale 0-5 in the released Phase 3) over re-extracted CommonCrawl pages that were the supervised training labels for the FineMath classifier.
Training annotations for the FineMath classifier (5-point math-quality scale, Phase 3) were generated by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct.

description_variants: Stage-2 (Dolmino) midtraining mix for the OLMo-3 7B base release; 99.95B-token mixture of high-quality math/code/QA/thinking/instruction/PDF/web data.

Per aggregator+leaf rule: subset 'midtraining-data' of allenai/dolma3_longmino_mix-50B-1025 folds the OLMo-3 7B stage-2 midtraining mix (allenai/dolma3_dolmino_mix-100B-1025) back into the long-context mix at 33.0B (66.1%); resolves to the dolmino 100B-1025 aggregator.
Stage 2 midtraining uses DataMix.OLMo_midtraining_mix_0625_100B, the dolma3-dolmino 100B annealing mixture, on 128 H100 GPUs.

OLMo-3 7B base was midtrained on allenai/dolma3_dolmino_mix-100B-1025 (Stage 2 Mid-training, 100B tokens; mix is 20% code / 28% web pages / 19% math / 14% QA / 8% thinking / 6% instruction / 5% PDFs).

Olmo 3 Base 7B is midtrained (Stage 2) on 100B high-quality tokens sampled from the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix (100B-1025), a brand-new midtraining data pool introduced in this work that combines novel synthetic math/code/QA/thinking/instruction sources with quality-filtered web and PDF data.
Stage-2 midtraining mix is Dolma 3 Dolmino (100B 1025 mix); README confirms 'dolma3-dolmino'.
7B midtraining uses the 100B-token aggregator Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix as its full training data pool.
Olmo-3-1025-7B was midtrained on the 100B-token 1025-dated released subset of Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix (allenai/dolma3_dolmino_mix-100B-1025), totaling 99.95B tokens across 236M docs.

Stage-2 midtraining on the 100B-token dolma3-dolmino mix (OLMo-midtraining-mix-0625-100B.txt) bundling Dolmino-math, Dolmino-FLAN, CraneCode, CraneMath, MegaMath, MegaMath rewrites, OLMoOCR PDFs, OpenMathReasoning rewrites, OpenThoughts2, QwQ/R1/Gemini/Llama-Nemotron reasoning traces, Tulu-3 SFT, verifiable-gpt41/o4mini, stem-heavy-crawl, stack-edu FIM buckets, TinyMATH, etc.
Olmo 3 Base 7B midtrained on Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix (100B tokens, 1025 release).
66% of the Longmino mix was carried over from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix midtraining data to preserve short-context performance.
Olmo 3 Base (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B) midtraining (Stage 2) consumes 100B high-quality tokens sampled from the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.
Stage 2 mid-training of Olmo-3-1025-7B on the Dolma 3 Dolmino mix (100B tokens). Mix composition reported as 20% code, 28% web pages, 19% math, 14% QA, 8% thinking, 6% instruction, and 5% PDFs.
Stage-2 midtraining mix DataMix.OLMo_midtraining_mix_0625_100B is the 100B-token dolma3-dolmino mixture (synthetic math/code, STEM crawl, reasoning traces), tokenized with allenai/dolma2-tokenizer.
Olmo 3 Base 7B was midtrained (Stage 2) on Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix, a 100B-token aggregator combining novel synthetic math/code/QA/thinking data with high-quality filtered web/PDF data.
Olmo 3 Base 7B's long-context extension mixes 66% high-quality short-context data sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix to preserve short-context performance.
Stage-2 midtraining uses the Dolma 3 Dolmino mid-training corpus (the OLMo-midtraining-mix-0625-100B mix sampled from the Dolma 3 Dolmino pool).
Midtraining (Stage 2) of Olmo 3 Base 7B uses 100B high-quality tokens sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix to improve key fundamental capabilities (math, code, QA, instruction, thinking).

The Longmino Mix used for Olmo 3 Base 7B Stage 3 long-context extension interleaves 66% short-context tokens sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix (~33B of the 50B-token extension), so the 7B model is exposed to Dolmino-Mix data during long-context training.
Stage 2 midtraining of Olmo 3 7B used a 100B-token sample from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix, the aggregator that combines synthetic math/code/QA/thinking/instruction data with high-quality web and PDF data.
Olmo-3-1025-7B was midtrained for 100B tokens on Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix, a newly introduced midtraining data pool combining novel synthetic sources with quality-filtered pretraining data.
34% of the long-context training mix is high-quality short-context data sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix; this prevents short-context degradation during the 7B's long-context extension.
Stage 2 midtraining data is sampled from dolma3-dolmino (Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix family).
Stage 2 (midtraining) trains on the Dolma 3 Dolmino mix (high-quality, targeted data). README identifies the stage 2 dataset as `dolma3-dolmino` (allenai/dolma3_dolmino).
OLMo 3 Base 7B's Stage 2 (midtraining) trains on 100B tokens sampled from the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix aggregator dataset.
Olmo 3 7B Base ingests the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix as the 66% short-context portion of the Stage 3 long-context training mix, ensuring short-context performance is preserved during context extension.

Leaf component of Dolma 3 Longmino Mix: 33.0B tokens (66.1% of the 50B Longmino Mix) of short-context midtraining data sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix, interleaved to prevent degradation on short-context tasks.

The 34% short-context portion of the Dolma 3 Longmino 50B mix (33.0B tokens, 66.1%) is sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix to prevent regression on short-context tasks during Olmo-3-1025-7B Stage 3 long-context extension.
Olmo-3-1025-7B is midtrained on 100B tokens sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix (Stage 2 of base training).
Aggregator: 100B-token Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix used for Stage 2 midtraining of Olmo 3 7B Base.
Short-context portion (66%, 33B tokens) of Olmo 3 7B Stage 3 training is sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.
Stage-2 midtraining draws from the dolma3-dolmino pool of synthetic math, code, STEM crawl, and reasoning traces.

description_variants: Stage-3 long-context extension training mix for OLMo-3 32B; 100B-token mixture of long-context PDF subsets (LC-s2pdf REX/CWE 32k-64k, LC-s2pdf 32k-64k, 8k-32k variants) and stage-2 midtraining data folded back in.

Long-context extension trains on DataMix.OLMo_longmino_mix_0925 (the dolma3_longmino_mix-100B-1125 aggregator) for 100B tokens at sequence_length=65536 with YaRN RoPE factor=8 and intra-document masking on 1024 H100 GPUs.

Olmo-3-1125-32B Stage-3 long-context training on the Dolma 3 Longmino 100B mix (Nov-2025 release). 100B tokens of mid-training data plus PDF long-context content. Final 32B base checkpoint is the merge of 4 final checkpoints from this stage.
Stage-3 corpus is the Dolma 3 Longmino 100B 1125 mix.

The 32B long-context extension trains for 100B tokens on Dolma 3 Longmino Mix (100B variant), maintaining the same 34/66 proportion of long-context olmOCR data to short-context Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix as the 50B mix.
Stage 3 of Olmo-3-1125-32B uses the 100B-token Dolma 3 Longmino Mix (proportions match the 7B mix).

The 32B's long-context extension uses the 100B-token leaf variant of Dolma 3 Longmino Mix (allenai/dolma3_longmino_mix-100B-1125), maintaining the same proportions as the 50B 7B mix (66% long-context, 34% short-context).
32B LC stage trained on OLMo-longmino-mix-0925 (HF release: allenai/dolma3_longmino_mix-100B-1125) for ~100B tokens.

Stage 3 long-context extension corpus (100B tokens) for Olmo-3-1125-32B; this aggregator preserves the 50B mix proportions, interleaving length-bucketed olmOCR PDFs with short-context data from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.
Long-context mix also includes a midtraining-data subset (replayed midtraining content) to maintain skills while extending context.

Leaf edge for the 'midtraining-data' sub-corpus of allenai/dolma3_longmino_mix-100B-1125, used in Stage-3 long-context training of Olmo-3-1125-32B per the aggregator+leaf rule (lattice subsets[] entry).
100B-token long-context mix for Olmo 3 32B (same proportions as the 50B 7B mix per Table 11).

edges.description: Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct produced the CraneCode subset of the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix by applying the SwallowCode SGCR style-rewrite and SCOR code-optimization stages to filtered Python files from The Stack v2 Smol.

anchor_list.excerpt: The rewriting pipeline, powered by Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, enhances code quality through stylistic and semantic transformations
anchor_list.explanation: Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct is the rewriter that produced SwallowCode (the dataset Qwen2-7B trains on); the paper applies the same SwallowCode corpus across both Llama-3.1-8B and Qwen2-7B runs.

description_variants: The SwallowCode component of the training mix was produced by rewriting bigcode/the-stack-v2-train-smol-ids Python snippets with Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct in the two-stage SGCR (Style-Guided) and SCOR (Self-Contained Optimization) rewriting pipeline; ~20.8M samples were rewritten at an estimated 23,703 H100 GPU-hours.

The SwallowMath component of the training mix was produced by rewriting FineMath-4+ with Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct using a five-step rewriting prompt (Appendix F): remove web headers/footers/privacy notices, drop irrelevant metadata, fill missing context, rewrite derivations concisely, present clear step-by-step solutions.
Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct rewrote the raw the-stack-v2-train-smol-ids Python snippets via the SGCR prompt (10 Google-Python-Style-Guide-derived criteria) to produce the exp10 corpus.

Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct rewrote FineMath-4+ documents using a five-step math rewriting prompt (Appendix F) to produce the SwallowMath corpus on which Llama-3.1-8B was continually pretrained in math exp2.

Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct rewrote the Python snippets used in Llama-3.1-8B's CPT, performing both Style-Guided Code Rewriting (SGCR; enforce Google Python Style Guide) and Self-Contained Optimization Rewriting (SCOR; ensure self-containment, optimize algorithms, transform trivial snippets into educational examples). Content originated from The-Stack-v2-train-smol-ids, not from Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, so this is transformation, not generation.

Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct executed the SwallowMath rewriting pipeline (boilerplate removal, context restoration, step-by-step explanation rewriting). Content originated from FineMath-4+, so Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct rewrote rather than generated.

Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct rewrote The-Stack-v2-train-smol-ids Python code into SwallowCode via two LLM stages: Style-Guided Code Rewriting (SGCR) enforcing Google Python Style Guide and Self-Contained Optimization Rewriting (SCOR) for self-containment and algorithmic improvement. Llama-3.1-8B then trained on the rewritten output.

Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct rewrote FineMath-4+ documents into SwallowMath (boilerplate removal, context restoration, concise step-by-step explanation reformatting). Llama-3.1-8B's continual pretraining math data consists of this rewritten content.

The math content Llama-3.1-8B trained on (tokyotech-llm/swallow-math) was produced by rewriting FineMath-4+ documents (originally from HuggingFaceTB/finemath, 9.6B tokens) with meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct: boilerplate removal, context restoration, and step-by-step explanation reformatting in Markdown/LaTeX.

The Python content Llama-3.1-8B trained on (tokyotech-llm/swallow-code) was rewritten in two LLM stages by meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct: Style-Guided Code Rewriting (SGCR) enforces Google Python Style Guide criteria and Self-Contained Optimization Rewriting (SCOR) makes snippets self-contained, optimized, and educational. Original code originated in bigcode/the-stack-v2-train-smol-ids.

The Python code Llama-3.1-8B trains on (SwallowCode v1) was produced by rewriting bigcode/the-stack-v2-train-smol-ids with Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct in two LLM-driven stages: Style-Guided Code Rewriting (SGCR, Google Python Style Guide adherence) and Self-Contained Optimization Rewriting (SCOR, dependency inlining and algorithmic optimization).

The math text Llama-3.1-8B trains on (SwallowMath v1) was produced by rewriting FineMath-4+ with Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct: removing residual web headers/footers/privacy notices, eliminating Q&A timestamps, restoring missing question/answer context, rewriting derivation steps to be concise, and providing step-by-step solutions.

The SwallowCode content that Llama-3.1-8B trained on originated as Python files from The-Stack-v2-train-smol-ids and was rewritten by Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct in a two-stage LLM rewriting pipeline (Style-Guided Code Rewriting following the Google Python Style Guide, then Self-Contained Optimization Rewriting that makes snippets self-contained and algorithmically efficient).

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: Real-user assistant turns in WildChat (the corpus WildLlama fine-tunes on) include responses generated by OpenAI's gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 (March 1, 2023 snapshot of gpt-3.5-turbo). Token-cost accounting and the per-model token-count keys in count_cost.py enumerate this dated snapshot among the OpenAI models served on the WildChat platform.

anchor_list.explanation: count_cost.py iterates WildChat turns and computes per-model token costs, enumerating gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 as a dated GPT-3.5-Turbo snapshot whose generations appear in WildChat.
Table 2 lists gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 as a generator of 24.96% of WildChat conversations; Figure 1 shows it active April–June 2023, within WildLlama's data cutoff.

description_variants: Real-user assistant turns in WildChat (the corpus WildLlama fine-tunes on) include responses generated by OpenAI's gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 (March 1, 2023 snapshot of gpt-3.5-turbo). Token-cost accounting and the per-model token-count keys in count_cost.py enumerate this dated snapshot among the OpenAI models served on the WildChat platform.

WildLlama's training conversations include chatbot responses generated by OpenAI's gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 snapshot, which was the active gpt-3.5-turbo pointer when AllenAI's chatbot service launched on April 9, 2023 and dominated traffic April–June 2023 (Figure 1) — the bulk of WildLlama's pre-July-16 training window. 24.96% of all WildChat conversations come from this snapshot per Table 2.
The March 1, 2023 GPT-3.5 Turbo snapshot generated assistant responses for the early portion of WildChat (active Apr-Jun 2023 per Figure 1) that became part of WildLlama's SFT data.

WildLlama's training data includes chatbot responses generated by OpenAI's gpt-4-0314 snapshot, which served the GPT-4 chatbot service during April–June 2023 (Figure 1) within WildLlama's pre-July-16 training window. 7.10% of all WildChat conversations come from this snapshot per Table 2; the GPT-4-based service was suspended Jul–Oct 2023 for budget reasons.

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: OpenAI's omni-moderation-latest is invoked on every WildChat turn (utils.py:query_moderation) and its category outputs decide inclusion: the Full release excludes 'sexual/minors'-flagged conversations, and the standard (non-Full) release additionally excludes any conversation where moderation['flagged'] is True or detoxify toxicity exceeds 0.1. WildLlama (and any model fine-tuned on WildChat) therefore consumes a corpus whose composition is filtered by omni-moderation-latest decisions.

anchor_list.explanation: Names omni-moderation-latest as the model running per-turn moderation in WildChat construction.
release-time filter directly uses omni-moderation-latest's category outputs to drop WildChat conversations from both Full and standard releases.
anchor_list.position: no_minors filter at lines 1036-1051 in push_dataset

anchor_list.explanation: Pins the moderation classifier to OpenAI's omni-moderation-latest model, exact match for the OpenAI/omni-moderation-latest lattice leaf.

anchor_list.excerpt: if turn['content'] != '' and (turn['openai_moderation']['flagged'] or ...): turn_toxic = True ... ds = ds.filter(lambda ex: not ex["toxic"] ...) ... repo_id = f"allenai/WildChat-{repo_size_str}"
anchor_list.explanation: Documents the `flagged` field driving exclusion of conversations from the public WildChat release — the filtering decision.

description_variants: The omni-moderation-latest model labels every WildChat conversation turn; turns whose response carries flagged=True (or whose conversation contains any flagged turn) are excluded from the public allenai/WildChat-4.8M release (kept only in the gated -Full release). The model only decides inclusion — content is unchanged — which is the filtered_by signature.
edges.quality_reason: The anchors clearly establish omni-moderation-latest filtering the WildChat corpus, but WildLlama is never named in any anchor; the dependency is inferred indirectly from WildLlama training on WildChat, making the subject-object link one step removed from the evidence.

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: WildChat content in English is processed by the en_core_web_trf spaCy NER pipeline (configured in run_presidio_ner.py and consumed by Microsoft Presidio's AnalyzerEngine) to identify entity spans (PERSON, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PHONE_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD, URL, IP_ADDRESS, etc.). Spans surviving frequency / context filters are then anonymized in-place to placeholders like <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> by redact_PII.py, transforming the released WildChat text. WildLlama (and any model trained on WildChat) consumes this redacted variant of the content.

anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly registers the en_core_web_trf spaCy pipeline as the English NER backbone for Presidio's AnalyzerEngine in the PII redaction pass.

anchor_list.excerpt:
result = anonymizer.anonymize(
    text=text_to_anonymize,
    analyzer_results=analyzer_results_new,
    operators=operators
)
turn["content"] = result.text
turn['redacted'] = True

English PII spans in WildChat were detected by the English spaCy transformer pipeline (en_core_web_trf) and redacted before WildLlama saw the conversations. English is the dominant language in WildChat (~53% of turns; Table 5).

en_core_web_trf (English transformer-based spaCy pipeline on roberta-base) is loaded in the Presidio NLP engine for English (lang_code=English) and runs NER over English-language WildChat turns; detected PERSON / EMAIL / PHONE / CREDIT_CARD / URL / etc. spans drive the AnonymizerEngine to replace them with <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_*> placeholders, modifying the released turn content.
edges.quality_reason: The anchors confirm en_core_web_trf is used for PII redaction of English WildChat content, but WildLlama is not named in any anchor; the transformation applies to the WildChat dataset and WildLlama's dependency is an indirect inference from training on that corpus.

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: WildBench (allenai/WildBench) is the held-out evaluation benchmark for WildChat-trained models: utils.remove_wildbench loads wildbench_ids.json and excises every conversation reserved for WildBench from the WildChat training corpus before release. This decontamination is precisely the held-out role of an evaluation set, and WildLlama (the WildChat-fine-tuned Llama-2 family in the lattice) is the corresponding model whose training corpus is held out from WildBench.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Qwen3 was used as the rewriter that produced the MegaMatt synthetic-math midtraining sub-corpus consumed by allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B (filtered Megamath-Web-Pro post-June-2023 rewritten with Qwen3).

anchor_list.excerpt: we re-generate this dataset using open source models. Specifically, we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023, apply filtering as in Megamath-Web-Pro-Max, and rewrite it using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a). This yields 3.88B tokens of high-quality data, which we refer to as MegaMatt.
anchor_list.explanation: States Qwen3 was the rewriter used to produce MegaMatt from filtered Megamath-Web-Pro.

edges.quality_reason: The anchor clearly supports Qwen3 transforming Megamath-Web-Pro data into MegaMatt, but the anchor does not explicitly state that the 32B model (Olmo-3-1125-32B) used this same MegaMatt dataset rather than it only being documented for the 7B run.

edges.subject: allenai/dolma
edges.description: Dolma-Code's filtering pipeline borrowed StarCoder's heuristic rules (Li et al., 2023b): XML-template detection, HTML code-to-text ratio cutoff at 0.2, Java/JS/Python code-to-comment ratio bounds. StarCoderData is the canonical released artifact whose pipeline produced these rules.

anchor_list.excerpt: Quality filter: Removed documents matching any of the following Starcoder filters (Li et al., 2023b): Contains XML template code. HTML code-to-text ratio <= 0.2. Java, Javascript, Python code-to-comment ratio <= 0.01 or > 0.8.
anchor_list.explanation: Names StarCoder filtering pipeline (whose released dataset is bigcode/starcoderdata) as the inspiration for Dolma-Code's filters.

edges.subject: allenai/dolma
edges.description: Dolma's web-quality filtering applied the full Gopher rules from Rae et al. (2021) — repetition rules, n-gram-character thresholds, word-length and stopword-set checks — that were originally introduced to clean MassiveText, the Gopher pretraining corpus. The Gopher rules removed 15.23% of UTF-8 characters in Dolma-Web.

anchor_list.excerpt: Remove any document that does not pass any of the Gopher rules (Rae et al., 2021) (15.23% of characters tagged for removal).
anchor_list.explanation: Names the Gopher-paper data-recipe (associated with MassiveText) as the methodology Dolma's web-quality filter borrowed.

edges.subject: allenai/dolma
edges.description: Dolma's web-quality filter borrowed the 'C4 NoPunc' heuristic from C4 (Raffel et al., 2020) — a single rule that removes paragraphs whose lines do not end in '.', '?', '!', or '"' (22.73% of UTF-8 characters tagged in Dolma-Web). The team explicitly chose this single rule over adopting the full C4 ruleset.

anchor_list.excerpt: we approach quality filtering by combining heuristics introduced by Gopher and C4. ... we keep all the Gopher rules (Gopher All) and keep a single heuristic from C4 designed to remove paragraphs that do not end in punctuation (C4 NoPunc).
anchor_list.explanation: Names the C4 dataset's filtering heuristic (NoPunc) as a methodology Dolma borrowed for its web-quality filter.

edges.subject: allenai/dolma
edges.description: Dolma's web-subset design (combining Common Crawl and C4) followed the data recipe used by Llama 2 (Touvron et al., 2023b). Llama 2's pretraining was 2T tokens with similar source-mix conventions; Dolma explicitly adopted that approach.

anchor_list.excerpt: Similar to data recipes for LLaMA and Llama 2, we supplement our web subset with C4 (Raffel et al., 2020).
anchor_list.explanation: Cites Llama 2's recipe as inspiration for Dolma's web composition.
